015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client.jetty;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.rest.client.*;
020
021/**
022 * {@link HttpTransportProvider} implementation that supplies a {@link JettyHttpTransport}.
023 *
024 * <p>
025 * Registered via {@code META-INF/services/org.apache.juneau.rest.client.HttpTransportProvider} so that
026 * {@code RestClient} can auto-discover Jetty 12 when this module is on the classpath.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
030 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
031 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
032 * (and possibly earlier).
033 *
034 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
035 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
036 * </ul>
037 *
038 * @since 9.2.1
039 */
040@SuppressWarnings({
041   "resource" // Provider methods return Closeables owned by the caller; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
042})
043public final class JettyHttpTransportProvider implements HttpTransportProvider {
044
045   @Override /* HttpTransportProvider */
046   public int getPriority() {
047      return 75;
048   }
049
050   @Override /* HttpTransportProvider */
051   public boolean isAvailable() {
052      try {
053         Class.forName("org.eclipse.jetty.client.HttpClient");
054         return true;
055      } catch (ClassNotFoundException e) {
056         return false;
057      }
058   }
059
060   @Override /* HttpTransportProvider */
061   @SuppressWarnings({
062      "java:S112" // Re-wrap as RuntimeException - JettyHttpTransport.create() throws generic Exception from Jetty HttpClient startup; no specific type fits and the HttpTransportProvider.create() SPI method cannot declare checked exceptions.
063   })
064   public HttpTransport create() {
065      try {
066         return JettyHttpTransport.create();
067      } catch (Exception e) {
068         throw new RuntimeException("Failed to create JettyHttpTransport: " + e.getMessage(), e);
069      }
070   }
071}
